---
title: "China’s DeepSeek to make permanent 75% price cut on flagship V4‑Pro AI model: How Nvidia may be the reaso - The Times of India"

DeepSeek, a Chinese AI company, has announced a dramatic 75% permanent price cut on its flagship V4-Pro AI model, a move that could significantly reshape the competitive landscape in the AI market. This decision comes amid increasing scrutiny of Nvidia's dominance in the AI hardware sector, suggesting that DeepSeek is positioning itself to capture market share by making its technology more accessible.

The V4-Pro, which is designed for advanced AI applications, will now be available at a fraction of its previous cost, potentially attracting a broader range of customers, from startups to established enterprises. This price reduction could challenge Nvidia's pricing strategy, especially as the demand for AI capabilities continues to surge across various industries. DeepSeek's CEO has indicated that this shift is not just about price but also about democratizing access to powerful AI tools, allowing more companies to innovate without the prohibitive costs often associated with high-performance AI systems.

For users, this means greater access to advanced AI technologies, which could lead to accelerated innovation and competition in the market. Competitors may need to reassess their pricing and product strategies to remain relevant, particularly as DeepSeek's move could set a precedent for other companies looking to lower prices in response to market pressures. 

As the AI landscape evolves, it will be crucial to monitor how this price cut affects competition and whether other players will follow suit in an effort to maintain their market positions.
